articles.title: CAIT calls for more GST simplification despite soaring tax collections
articles.description: CAIT has demanded further simplification of the GST regime. While revenue has surged from Rs 40,000 crore to over Rs 2 lakh crore since 2017, traders face ground-level compliance challenges, hindering the ease of doing business in India.

articles.title: Carrot extract can make fake ghee dodge quality test: study
articles.description: Study reveals carrot extract enables fake ghee to pass quality tests, raising concerns about fraud in India's dairy market.

articles.description: A 2,495 sq ft newly restored home in San Francisco is listed for about $2,995,000 (Rs 28.7 crore), and the buyer has listed OpenAI and Anthropic shares as payment options.

articles.title: Milma milk price to go up by ₹4 a litre from Monday
articles.description: Milma milk prices will rise by ₹4 per litre from Monday due to increased production costs and low returns for farmers.

articles.title: Rayagada Railway Division starts June 1: What it means for passengers, routes and train operations
articles.description: Rayagada railway division latest news: The new Rayagada Railway Division begins operations on June 1. Here's what it means for passengers, train routes, railway administration and train services.

articles.title: Japanese proverbs about friendship: 'If the fish is kind to the water, the water is kind to the fish'
articles.description: This Japanese proverb, "If the fish is kind to the water, the water is kind to the fish," emphasizes the importance of reciprocity in relationships. It highlights that mutual respect, kindness, and effort are the foundations for healthy friendships and connections, fostering an environment where trust and care can flourish.

articles.title: Why are 85% of finance graduates chasing the same jobs? Edelweiss MF CEO highlights talent gap in high-growth roles
articles.description: Radhika Gupta, CEO of Edelweiss Mutual Fund, calls for wider career awareness in finance. She highlights that campuses overemphasize roles like equity research, while areas like risk management and sales offer significant future opportunities. Gupta advises students to consider future skill demand and lower competition. The focus should be on long-term career journeys, not just initial placements.

articles.title: ET Alpha Wealth Summit: Will AI disrupt India’s growth story or accelerate it?
articles.description: Indian market is at a key inflection point as strong macroeconomic fundamentals support long-term growth, while rapid advances in artificial intelligence raise fresh questions on jobs, industries and investment opportunities. The ET Alpha Wealth Summit in Mumbai will host a major debate on whether India’s next decade will be defined by Amritkaal-led growth or AI-led disruption.

articles.title: Annapurna Bhandar Yojana in West Bengal: Rs 3,000 to be credited – How to link Aadhaar with bank account
articles.description: The West Bengal government has launched the Annapurna Bhandar Yojana, under which eligible women will receive Rs 3,000 per month through Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT). To receive the financial assistance, beneficiaries must ensure their Aadhaar is linked to their bank account. Here's a step-by-step guide to complete the process.

articles.title: Coffee Day Global returns to profit in FY26, revenue up 5.7% at ₹1,094 cr
articles.description: Coffee Day Global Ltd, the operator of popular cafe chain Cafe Coffee Day, reported a profit after tax (PAT) of Rs 14 crore in FY26, while its revenue from operations grew 5.7 per cent to Rs 1,094 crore. The company's operating profit or EBITDA rose 27 per cent to Rs 198 crore in FY26, according to an exchange filing by its parent entity, Coffee Day Enterprises Ltd (CDEL). In FY25, Coffee Day Global had reported a net revenue of Rs 1,034.63 crore and a loss after tax of Rs 175.92 crore. Coffee Day Global's Average Sales Per Day (ASPD) witnessed a marginal increase to Rs 21,101 in the financial year ended March 31, 2026, compared with Rs 21,016 in FY25. However, the number of Cafe Coffee Day outlets declined to 424 at the end of FY26 from 435 a year earlier. Its Same Store Sales Growth (SSSG) stood at negative 1.72 per cent during the fiscal. On the other hand, the company's vending machine business expanded, with the count rising to 55,802 by the end of the fourth quarter of FY26,

articles.title: Suzlon to challenge SEBI's Rs 28.6 crore penalty, plans SAT appeal
articles.description: Suzlon plans to challenge SEBI's Rs 28.6-crore penalty order before SAT, arguing that the regulator had reversed an earlier 2025 adjudication order that had closed proceedings without imposing any penalty.

articles.title: CBSE says vulnerabilities in OSM portal OnMark contained
articles.description: The Central Board of Secondary Education has contained vulnerabilities in its OnMark portal. Cybersecurity experts from government agencies and IITs are working to secure the platform. They are fortifying systems and eliminating any remaining exploitable weaknesses. The identified issues have been addressed. The board is ensuring a more secure setup for its services.

articles.title: Suzlon Energy to challenge Sebi's order imposing ₹29 cr penalty in SAT
articles.description: Suzlon Energy Ltd said it will move the Securities Appellate Tribunal (SAT) against a Sebi order imposing penalty of Rs 28.95 crore on the company, its two promoters and ex-CFOs over alleged misrepresentation of financial statements. In a BSE filing, the company stated that the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Sebi), through an order passed on May 29, 2026, set aside an earlier adjudication ruling on June 27, 2025. The earlier ruling had exonerated Suzlon Energy and its promoter-directors Vinod R Tanti, Girish R Tanti and former CFOs Kirti J Vagadi and Amit Agarwal without imposing any penalty regarding allegations of misrepresentation in financial statements between FY14 and FY18. "The company will be filing an appeal before the Securities Appellate Tribunal in respect of the SEBI order dated 29th May 2026," the renewable energy solutions provider said on Saturday. Capital markets regulator Sebi imposed a penalty of Rs 15.95 crore on Suzlon Energy Ltd (SEL), Rs 5.75 crore o

articles.title: Economic fundamentals favour a stronger rupee, reforms needed for higher growth: Economist Surjit Bhalla
articles.description: Calling for immediate reforms, Bhalla urged the government to permanently rule out retrospective taxation. Describing the practice as wrong morally, wrong in principle, he said such measures hurt investor confidence and private investment.

articles.title: FPI profile: Foreign investors continue selling spree in May, pull out Rs 32,000 crore
articles.description: Foreign portfolio investors continued their exit from Indian equities in May, withdrawing Rs 32,963 crore due to concerns over earnings growth and a weakening rupee. This outflow has pushed cumulative FPI withdrawals in 2026 to Rs 2.25 lakh crore, surpassing the entire previous year's figure. While selling intensity has eased, a turnaround in flows is unlikely without significant macroeconomic improvement.

articles.title: Shivraj Chouhan to launch 'Khet Bachao Abhiyan' on June 1 as Govt reviews fertiliser, fuel stocks amid West Asia crisis
articles.description: Union Agriculture Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan launches Khet Bachao Abhiyan nationwide from June 1. The campaign aims to reduce excessive fertiliser use and promote balanced application. It will run until June 30, starting from Raisen, Madhya Pradesh. The minister will visit farms across the country. This initiative supports the Prime Minister's call for reduced fertiliser consumption and improved soil health.

articles.title: NIA conducts searches across three south Indian states in Kerala illegal explosives seizure case
articles.description: The National Investigation Agency (NIA) conducted searches across Kerala, Tamil Nadu, and Karnataka. These searches are part of an ongoing investigation into a large seizure of explosives in Malappuram, Kerala. Several accused persons have been arrested. The agency is working to uncover the full supply chain of these illegally procured explosives.

articles.title: Zepto shares crash 30% in unlisted market despite Sebi nod for IPO. What's cooking?
articles.description: Zepto’s unlisted shares have dropped sharply despite Sebi approval for its IPO, signalling investor caution in a volatile market. The decline reflects broader weakness in pre-IPO valuations, macro uncertainty and funding pressures, even as the quick commerce firm prepares for a high-profile public issue amid intense competition.

articles.title: Choose your FD scheme: Fixed deposits vs Post Office deposits -- which offers better returns in June 2026?
articles.description: Fixed deposits and post office savings schemes remain two of India's most popular low-risk investment options, offering predictable returns and capital protection. While bank FDs score on flexibility and liquidity, post office schemes often provide higher sovereign-backed returns, especially for long-term investors and senior citizens.
